tasks.label: Git: Create Feature Branch
tasks.command: set -e; git checkout main && git pull && git checkout -b ${input:branchType}/${input:branchSlug} && echo '' && echo '✓ Branch created: '${input:branchType}/${input:branchSlug} && echo 'You are now on the new branch. Start coding!'

tasks.label: Git: Create Pull Request
tasks.command:
set -e; BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD); [ "$BRANCH" = 'main' ] && { echo 'ERROR: You are on main. Switch to a feature/fix/chore branch first.'; exit 1; } || true; git push -u origin "$BRANCH"; gh pr create --base main --head "$BRANCH" --title "${input:prTitle}" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

<!-- Describe what this PR does and why -->

## Test plan

- [ ] `uv run pre-commit run --all-files` passes
- [ ] Relevant tests added or updated

🤖 Created via VS Code task
EOF
)" && echo '' && echo '✓ Pull request created.'

tasks.label: Git: Create Release
tasks.command: set -e; git checkout main && git pull; git status --porcelain | grep -q . && { echo 'ERROR: working tree is not clean. Commit or stash changes first.'; exit 1; } || true; VER=${input:releaseVersion}; grep -qF "## [$VER]" CHANGELOG.md || { echo "ERROR: CHANGELOG.md has no '## [$VER]' section. Update and commit the changelog first."; exit 1; }; TAG=v$VER; echo "Tagging $TAG on main..."; git tag -a "$TAG" -m "Release $TAG" && git push origin "$TAG" && echo '' && echo "✓ Tag $TAG pushed. The release workflow will run on GitHub."

inputs.description: PR title (must start with ADD / FIX / CHORE, e.g. ADD connection monitor)
Version to release (without leading 'v', e.g. 1.2.3 or 0.2.0-beta.1)
